summary: The repeated simulation failures are due to the DUT not matching the reference model's behavior for the don't-care input combinations (3, 8, 11, 12). The testbench expects deterministic logic-0 outputs for these cases, but the current RTL may produce X or incorrect values. To break the stagnation, this patch explicitly enumerates all 16 input combinations and assigns out_sop and out_pos to logic-1 only for 2, 7, and 15, and logic-0 for all others (including the don't-cares), ensuring deterministic outputs that match the reference model for every possible input.
rtl_lines: // Inputs as a 4-bit vector for easier minterm/maxterm mapping
// Explicitly enumerate all 16 input combinations for deterministic outputs
risks: Explicit case enumeration may be less optimal for synthesis than minimal SOP/POS, but guarantees deterministic outputs for all input combinations.
If the reference model expects a different value for the don't-care cases (unlikely per evidence), this patch may still mismatch.
next_steps: Run simulation to confirm that all outputs match the reference for all 16 input combinations.
If mismatches persist, probe the outputs for the don't-care cases to verify their values.
If synthesis optimization is required, revisit minimal SOP/POS forms but ensure deterministic outputs for all cases.
